steps.n: Adding emphatic نّ suffix for additional emphasis/assertion

steps.m: surely I will kill you (said by one brother to the other)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show who will be killed. This word appears in the story of Adam's two sons, where one brother is threatening the other.

steps.m: he said (subject of لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ - I will surely kill you)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This word shows who is speaking the next phrase لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ (I will surely kill you).
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before it and is between two moving letters (letters with vowels)
Adding shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Adding مَا changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This word restricts the meaning of the verb يَتَقَبَّلُ (accepts) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ت and doubling the middle letter makes the acceptance more intense and willing
Changes from past tense (he accepted) to present tense (he accepts) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: he accepts willingly/thoroughly (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Changes from 'a' to 'u' ending to show this is a normal present tense verb (not a command or wish)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'god' (إِلَه) became the name 'Allah' by adding ال. The doubled L sound (shadda) makes it special. The mark on top (ُ) shows Allah is doing the action in this sentence.
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word. This is a common change when مِن comes before ال
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'protected' to 'someone who makes effort to protect themselves', like adding a shield
Adds ين at the end to make it plural (more than one person)

steps.m: the ones who protect themselves (from sin)
steps.n: Makes the noun definite

steps.m: from/of the ones who protect themselves (from sin) (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Shows this word is the object of مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Starting with the basic conditional particle. The small ۢ mark (called tanwin) helps with pronunciation when connecting to the next word.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
The إِ changes to ئِ when it comes after لَ. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: you stretched out (addressing Cain in the story of Cain and Abel)
steps.n: The تَ at the end shows that 'you' (a male) did this action. In this ayah, this is part of Abel speaking to his brother Cain about if Cain were to stretch out his hand to kill him.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'towards' or 'to'
Added the pronoun 'me' (ي) which combines with the final ى of إلى, shown by the shadda mark. This shows who is being moved towards.
Connected the root letters together. The final ي drops because this is a weak root.

steps.m: your hand (object of بَسَطتَ - you stretched out)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show the hand belongs to 'you'. The َ before كَ shows this word is being acted upon by the verb بَسَطتَ (you stretched out).
Adds تَ at start to make it 'you' + present tense form of 'kill'
The لِ prefix adds the meaning of purpose/reason ('in order to')
Added نَّ for emphasis, showing the action will definitely happen

steps.m: so that you definitely kill me
steps.n: Added ي to show 'me' as the one being killed
This is the basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates the word بِبَاسِطٍ (one who extends/stretches).
The alif is elongated (آ) for emphasis in pronunciation. This is a common Quranic writing style that doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'I' when talking about yourself
Added alif at the end to complete the standard form of the word

Added a small mark (۠) above the final alif. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, this word is connected to بِبَاسِطٍ (one who extends), showing that 'I' am not going to extend my hand.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with/by one who stretches out (in relation to يَدِىَ - my hand)
steps.n: Added the -in sound (tanween) at the end to show this word connects to 'my hand' (يَدِىَ) in the sentence, telling us how the hand is not stretching out
Root letters joined together to make the simple word for 'hand'
Added ي with kasra to show possession (my)
Added fatha to show it's in a regular sentence position
This is the basic preposition meaning 'towards' or 'to'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كَ at the end to show this is directed 'towards you'. This word shows who the stretching (بَسَطتَ) is being directed towards.
Added vowel marks to make it 'he killed' in the past tense
Changes 'he killed' to 'I kill' by adding أ at start to show 'I' and changing vowels for present tense

steps.m: so that I kill/in order to kill
steps.n: Added لِ (li-) at start to mean 'so that/in order to' - shows purpose
Adds the object (you) to show who is being killed
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
The ن doubles and gets a kasra because 'I' (ي) is attached to it, making it mean 'Indeed I'

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Adding ي with an alif after it (ىٓ) shows this is about 'me/I'. This word connects to the next word أَخَافُ (I fear)
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا when we add the vowels.

steps.m: I fear (the subject of this fear is اللَّهَ - Allah)
steps.n: The أَ at the beginning shows 'I' and the ُ at the end shows this is happening now. This word connects to اللَّهَ (Allah) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال and merged it with the word, doubling the ل with a shadda. The أ changed to ٱ at the start. This makes it a proper name for The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared in إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being feared in the phrase 'Indeed, I fear Allah'

steps.m: to care for and help grow
he cared for and helped grow
steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

Adding shadda combines the two ب letters and makes the meaning stronger, showing complete and perfect nurturing. This word is connected to العٰلَمِينَ (all worlds/beings) showing that Allah is the Lord of everything.
Added alif to create a noun meaning 'that which is known' - referring to all of creation as something that can be known or understood
Added ين to make it plural, showing there are many worlds or realms in creation

steps.m: the worlds (referring to all realms of creation)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the worlds' instead of just 'worlds', and changed how the first A is written
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed I (subject of أُرِيدُ - I want)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me'. This word connects to أُرِيدُ (I want) later in the verse, showing who is doing the wanting.
Changed the writing style of ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it. This doesn't change the meaning, it's just a different way of writing the same sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
This is a connecting word that shows the second action (تَبُوٓأَ - you bear) is what is wanted/intended by the first action (أُرِيدُ - I want). It's like saying 'I want that you bear...'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from active (he settled) to passive (was settled) by changing the middle vowel
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: that you bear/take upon yourself (connected to أُرِيدُ أَن)
steps.n: The long vowel mark (ـوٓ) and final vowel change show this verb is connected to 'I want that you...' (أُرِيدُ أَن), making it part of a bigger wish/desire

steps.m: to commit a wrong or sin

steps.n: Root meaning 'sin' becomes the noun 'a sin' by adding i- sound at start and making middle letter silent
Added بِ (bi) meaning 'with' or 'by' in front of the word sin
Added ي to show it's my sin. This word is connected to تَبُوٓأَ (you bear/carry) in the ayah, showing what is being carried.
Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This makes it name the action instead of describing doing it.
Added conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and your sin (object of تَبُوٓأَ - 'that you bear')
steps.n: Added كَ (your) at the end to show this sin belongs to the person being spoken to. The word is being acted upon by the verb تَبُوٓأَ (that you bear/carry).
Added the vowels 'u' (damma) to make the root letters into a basic verb meaning 'to be'
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Changed from independent verb (you are) to dependent verb (that you will be) by changing the last vowel

steps.m: and then you will be (result of أُرِيدُ - I want)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this happens as a result of the previous action (I want)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَصْحٰبِ (companions/people) to show 'from the people'.

steps.m: to accompany or be close to someone/something

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it 'he accompanied' in the past tense
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple companions
Added kasra at end to show this word is connected to 'the Fire' that comes after it
The root letters change to make the noun 'fire' - the middle letter و becomes ا and we add the vowel َ (fatha)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda

steps.m: of the fire (object of مِنْ أَصْحٰبِ/among the companions)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows مِنْ أَصْحٰبِ (among the companions of)
This is the basic demonstrative word that points to something. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's a longer sound.
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something close ('this') to pointing to something farther away ('that')
Adding كَ makes the word complete and emphasizes that we're pointing to something specific

steps.m: and that (referring to the punishment mentioned)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it. Here it's connecting to the previous statement about becoming one of the companions of the Fire.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changed the word into a noun meaning the result of the action
Changed spelling to show long 'aa' sound and added 'u' ending since it's doing the action
Added alif at the end to make it plural
Changes from the basic verb 'to wrong' into 'wrongdoer' by adding alif after first letter and kasra before last letter - like changing 'write' to 'writer'
The shadda emphasizes the meaning
Adding ين makes it plural (more than one wrongdoer)
Adding ال makes it specific. The final َ shows it belongs to جَزٰٓؤُا۟ (reward/recompense) in the ayah.
Added marks and doubled middle letter to change from 'obeying' to 'making someone/something obey'
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female did this action. This word is connected to قَتْلَ (killing) in the ayah, showing what she made easy.
Added فَ at the start to show this happened as a result of something before it
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: to/for him (connected to نَفْسُ - his soul)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) with damma to show it's connected to him. This word connects to نَفْسُ (soul) in the ayah to show whose soul is being talked about.

steps.m: to/for him (connected to نَفْسُ - his soul)
steps.n: Added the elongation sign (ۥ) after هُ to show the long vowel sound

steps.m: to breathe, to be precious, to desire

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence - it's the subject of طَوَّعَتْ (made appealing)

steps.m: his self (as the doer of طَوَّعَتْ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'his' to show whose self it is. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel.
Changed from action to noun form to show the act of killing itself. This word is connected to أَخِيهِ (his brother) showing what was killed.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
The root letters combine to form the basic noun 'brother'. The weak letter و drops in this form.
The kasra at the end prepares the word to show possession
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my brother'
Adding هِ shows the brother belongs to him. This word is being acted upon by the word قَتْلَ (killing) in the phrase قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ (killing of his brother).
Added fathas to make it past tense, meaning 'he killed'
Added فَ to connect this action to what happened before it in the story
Added هُ at the end to show who was killed (him)
Adding هُ to indicate 'him' as object
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed verb to express entering into a state or condition (not just about morning anymore)

steps.m: and so he became (one of الْخٰسِرِينَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the previous action (killing his brother). This word connects to الْخٰسِرِينَ (the losers) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects to الخٰسِرِينَ (the losers) to show someone is part of that group.
Added fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الخٰسِرِينَ. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Adding an alif after the first letter changes it from an action to someone who does that action
The ين ending makes it plural and shows it's affected by the word مِنَ (from/of) that comes before it
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific ('the losers' instead of just 'losers'). The long alif (ٰ) is a spelling variation of ا.

steps.m: to send forth, to raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to يَبْحَثُ (searches) in the ayah, showing who sent the raven.

steps.m: and then he sent forth (subject of يَبْحَثُ)
steps.n: The فَ at the start shows this happened as a result or sequence after something mentioned before. Here it shows Allah's response to the situation.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of بَعَثَ - sent)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and changed to the special form used only for Allah. The word Allah is the subject (doer) of the verb بَعَثَ (sent) in this ayah.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning a specific type of creature
The ًا (an) at the end shows this is the object - Allah sent (بَعَثَ) a raven to show something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he searches (in the ground, referring to الغُرَابُ - the crow)
steps.n: The يَ at the start shows this is happening now. The crow is doing this action in the story. The ُ at the end shows the crow is the one doing the searching.

steps.m: in (shows where the غُرَاب is searching)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it shows where the crow (غُرَاب) is searching.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to make it readable as 'ard' (meaning earth)
Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because it follows the word فِي (in)
Added basic past tense vowels
Changed from past tense 'saw' to present tense 'shows' by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added لِ to show purpose - 'in order to/so that'
Added هُ suffix meaning 'him' as the object being shown to
This is a question word that always asks about the way something happens

steps.m: how (asking about the way the raven would bury يُوٰرِى)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the question word. In this ayah, it's asking about the way/manner in which the raven would bury something, connecting to the verb يُوٰرِى (buries) that comes after it.
Added vowels and alif to make 'he hid/covered' (past tense verb)
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'a bad thing' instead of 'to be bad'
Added ة to specify one instance of something shameful, here referring to private parts which are considered shameful to expose

steps.m: private part (object of فَأُوٰرِىَ - so I may cover)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being covered in the phrase 'so I may cover the private parts'
Connected the root letters and added fatha to make it a noun. The final weak letter و is dropped.
Added kasra to show this word will be possessed by someone
Added yaa at the end to mean 'my'

steps.m: his brother (possessed by سَوْءَةَ - dead body)
steps.n: Added هِ (hi) at the end to change from 'my brother' to 'his brother'. Connected to the word سَوْءَةَ (dead body) to mean 'dead body of his brother'.
Added fathas to make basic past tense form
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ because و changes to ا in hollow verbs
The basic word expressing intense distress or misfortune
Added ة to show one specific instance of distress
Changed to show possession (belonging to me) with ى at the end
Added يٰ at the beginning to show calling out in distress or regret
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing someone else to lack ability
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he' to 'I'

steps.m: I was unable (to be like هٰذَا ٱلْغُرَابِ - this raven)
steps.n: Changed from making something unable to being unable myself. The تُ at the end shows I did this action in the past. Links to 'being like this raven' in the ayah.

steps.m: that (connects to أَكُونَ - I would be)
steps.n: This is a special word that connects two ideas and shows that the second part (أَكُونَ - I would be) is the goal or result of the first part. Here it connects to his expression of regret about not being able to be like the crow.
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being
Changed the root letters to make the basic present tense verb by adding 'u' sounds on top
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
Added fatha at end to show this is in a special grammar position

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Added kasra under first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'similar to'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing what comes after it (the crow)
The base demonstrative word that points to something. Like pointing with your finger and saying 'that'
Added ه (shortened from ها) at the start to get attention and show we're pointing to something near
Added the alif mark (ٰ) above ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation
Changes from basic root into noun meaning the black bird
Added ال to show this is the specific raven that Allah sent
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows مِثْلَ (like)
Changes from verb (to hide) to noun (one who hides)
Added أُ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from something before it

steps.m: so that I may hide/cover (سَوْءَةَ أَخِي - my brother's corpse)
steps.n: Changed to show 'so that' meaning (after فَ) - like saying 'in order to hide' instead of just 'hide'

steps.m: to be bad, evil, or shameful

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun referring to something shameful

steps.m: a specific shameful thing (the dead body)
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance - here referring to the dead body that needs to be buried

steps.m: the shameful thing (the dead body of أَخِيهِ - his brother)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being buried (as shown by the verb يُوٰرِى meaning 'he buries')
The root letters are connected and the weak letter و is dropped to make the basic noun 'brother'
Adding kasra to prepare the word for showing possession
Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' brother
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added أَ at the beginning to show change of state

steps.m: and so he became (connected to يٰوَيْلَتَىٰٓ - 'woe to me' in previous phrase)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this happened as a result of the previous action
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word. This shows he became one of those who regret.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than two people)
Added ال (the) at the beginning to make it 'the regretful ones' instead of just 'regretful ones'

steps.m: among the ones who feel sorry
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah

steps.m: from/because of (connects to أَجْلِ to mean 'because of')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that combines with the next word أَجْلِ to mean 'because of' or 'for the sake of'. Together they explain why Allah wrote something for the Children of Israel.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form a noun meaning time/deadline
Adding ِ at the end because it follows مِنْ. Together مِنْ أَجْلِ means 'because of' - literally 'from the time/cause of'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
The alif is replaced with a special mark (overbar) to show distance, changing 'this' to 'that'
Adding لِ emphasizes the distance, showing the thing is far away

steps.m: that (masculine, far) (points to مِنْ أَجْلِ - 'because of')
steps.n: Adding كَ shows this is masculine and completes the word. Here it points back to the phrase 'because of' that came before it.
This is the basic meaning showing the action of writing
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters spell 'he wrote' in the past tense
Changed the last vowel to sukun to prepare for adding نا
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a basic preposition showing the concept of being higher or above something
Adding ى at the end makes it work as a preposition that connects words
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the end of the word before connecting to the next word بَنِىٓ (children/tribe)
This root relates to building or establishing something. When used for people, it refers to those who are 'built from' or descended from someone.
Adding vowels turns the root letters into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he built'
The long vowel at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it (إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ), meaning 'children of Israel'
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew (Yisrael). It combines two parts: 'isra' meaning 'to strive/wrestle' and 'il' meaning 'God'. It refers to Prophet Jacob (يعقوب) who was given this name.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' at the start of a sentence in English.
Added هُۥ at the end which means 'he/it'. This connects to the word قَتَلَ (killed) in the ayah to show who did the action.
This is a special word that introduces a conditional statement about people - like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb قَتَلَ (killed) to mean 'whoever kills'
This is the basic meaning of killing or taking life

steps.m: he took a life (subject of مَن who/whoever)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word comes after مَن (whoever) in the ayah, showing who did the action.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning a single soul or person
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of being killed. Like saying 'killed a person' instead of just 'killed person'.

steps.m: to be different from, to change from

steps.n: Basic noun form meaning 'other than'
The ب at the beginning combines with غَيْر to create the meaning of 'without', while the ِ at the end shows it's connected to the word نَفْسٍ that follows it
Root letters ن-ف-س got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the noun 'nafs' meaning soul/person
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it's what is being killed in the phrase 'whoever kills a soul'.

steps.m: or (connecting قَتَلَ نَفْسًۢا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ with فَسَادٍ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects 'killing a soul without [killing] a soul' with 'corruption', showing these are two different reasons.
Added alif after second letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of that action
Added two dots under the last letter to show this word is receiving the action (like saying 'of corruption')
This is a basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it's used with الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something happens.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Basic root letters got vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make it a usable word meaning 'earth'

steps.m: the earth (in فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - in the earth)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it follows the word في (in/on)
Starting with the basic comparison particle ك
Adding أَنَّ makes it express similarity with emphasis
Adding ما makes the word mean 'as if it' instead of just 'as if' - it points to something specific

steps.m: then it is as if/so it is as though
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a completed action. This word follows مَن (whoever) in the ayah, showing who did the action.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed from a verb about being social into the common noun 'people' by adding alif
Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: the people (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters with vowel marks to show completeness. This word describes how the action of أَحْيَا (bringing to life) affects everyone.

steps.m: all together completely (describes أَحْيَا/brought to life)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how completely the action of bringing to life affects everyone
This is a relative pronoun that refers to any person (like 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English)
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb أَحْيَاهَا (saves/gives life to it) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to give life, to cause to live
steps.n: Added 'a' (hamza) at start to mean 'making something happen' - like changing 'live' to 'make live'

steps.m: gave life to her/it (referring to نَفْسًا - a soul)
steps.n: Added ها to show the object is feminine (referring back to نَفْسًا - 'a soul' mentioned earlier in the verse)
Starting with the basic comparison particle ك which shows similarity
Added أَنَّ to strengthen the comparison and add certainty
Added ما to make the comparison more general and hypothetical
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before, and lengthened the final alif
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

Added hamza with fatha (أَ) at the beginning to make it mean 'cause to live/give life'. The sukun (ْ) was added to keep proper pronunciation. This verb is connected to هَا (it/her) in the ayah, showing what received the life-giving action.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to be social' into a word meaning 'people' by dropping the first letter and adding ا in the middle. This is a common pattern for making nouns from roots.
Adding ال makes the word definite, like adding 'the' in English
Added fatha at the end to show these people are receiving the action of being killed in the phrase قَتَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ (he killed the people)

steps.m: to gather, collect, or bring together

steps.n: Adding ي makes it mean 'all together' instead of just 'gather'

steps.m: all together (describing how قَتَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ 'killed the people' happened)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how the action happened, like saying 'killed the people as one complete group'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the base word قد, which shows certainty or completion
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowels and changed spelling to show past tense. The آ (alif with madda) is used instead of اء to show a long 'aa' sound.
Added تْ at the end to show a feminine subject (she)

steps.m: she came to them (referring to رُسُلُنَا - our messengers)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who received the action (them). This refers to the messengers (رُسُلُنَا) mentioned in the next part of the verse.
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
The word changed from 'rasool' to 'rusul' by changing the middle vowel to make it plural - like changing 'book' to 'books' but in Arabic style
Added نَا at the end to show these are our messengers
Changed to show something is very obvious/clear (like adding 'very' in English)
The word changed to mean 'multiple clear signs/proofs' by adding -aat at the end (like adding -s in English). The -aat ending makes it feminine and shows there are many signs.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is being possessed by something after it
Added بِ (bi) at start to mean 'with/by the signs'
Basic particle that shows something happens after something else, with a gap in time between them

steps.m: then (after the messengers came to them)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda (ّ) on last letter to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows what happened after Allah's messengers came with clear signs.
Basic form of the conditional/uncertainty particle
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. This emphasizes the statement that many of them (كَثِيرًا) are transgressors.
Root letters got vowels to make it a descriptive word meaning 'much/many'
Added ي to make the quality stronger
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by إِنَّ (like adding 'a/an' in English)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English
The shadda is added because this word comes after a vowel sound in the ayah, making the م sound stronger

steps.m: from them (referring to بَنِىٓ إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ - Children of Israel)
steps.n: Adding هُم at the end shows this refers to 'them' (the Children of Israel mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to create a word meaning 'after' - this shows the time or order that comes later
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it in the sentence
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance, changing 'this' to 'that'
Added لِ to specify this is for masculine things

steps.m: that one (pointing to something far away)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to make it mean 'that one over there' - like pointing far away with your finger
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows corruption happening inside the earth.
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to make it readable as 'ard' (earth)
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it comes after the word فِي (in)

steps.m: one who goes beyond proper limits
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle makes it describe someone who does this action regularly

steps.m: those who go beyond proper limits
steps.n: Makes the word plural masculine nominative

steps.m: surely/truly they are those who go beyond proper limits
steps.n: Adding لَ at the beginning adds emphasis to show this is definitely true
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. Not derived from a root.
Adding ما at the end makes the meaning restrictive, like saying 'only' or 'nothing but' in English.

steps.m: to give what someone deserves (good or bad)

steps.n: Added fatha marks and connected the letters. The ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added hamza (ء) at the end to make it into a noun meaning the result of the action

steps.m: the just reward (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Changed to show it's the subject (doer) by adding ُ at the end, with ا and a small circle to complete the word. The hamza sits on و because of the damma sound before it.
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ with shadda to make it specific. This word describes the people who are doing the action يُحَارِبُونَ (they wage war against).
Added alif after first letter to show two sides fighting each other
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. This shows ongoing action against Allah and His Messenger.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who fight against Allah and His Messenger.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being fought against)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being fought against in this verse, along with His Messenger. Also added the shadda to emphasize the ل and changed the initial alif to a wasla since it connects with the previous word.
Basic meaning of sending or dispatching something
Changed from basic verb to noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'

steps.m: messenger (object of يُحَارِبُونَ - wage war against)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُحَارِبُونَ (wage war against)
Added هُ to show this is His (Allah's) messenger
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous word (Allah)
Basic meaning of movement or effort
Root letters joined with fatha vowels to make past tense 'he' form. Ends with ى because it's a special root ending.
Added يَ at start to show ongoing action in present/future
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و because of this ending.

steps.m: and they strive (connected to يُحَارِبُونَ - they wage war)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this action with the previous one (waging war against Allah and His messenger)
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This word connects with الْأَرْضِ to show location 'in the earth'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word فِى (in) that comes before it

steps.m: the earth (being acted upon by فِى)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - referring to THE earth rather than just any land
Creates a noun showing the result of corrupting or damaging. This word describes what the evildoers are trying to spread in the land, as shown by يَسْعَوْنَ (they strive) coming before it.
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of يَسْعَوْنَ (they strive), telling us what exactly they are striving to do - spread corruption.

steps.m: that (introduces what will happen to them)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects the punishment (جَزٰٓؤُا) with what the punishment will be (يُقَتَّلُوٓا۟). It works like 'that' in English when you say 'The punishment is that they will be...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows this happens many times
Adding يُ at the start changes it from 'he killed many times' to 'he is being killed many times' - shows someone is receiving the action

steps.m: they are being killed many times
steps.n: Added 'وا' at the end to show multiple people (they) are receiving the action

steps.m: they are being killed many times (completed)
steps.n: Added ا with special marks to show the word is complete
This is a conjunction that connects different possible punishments in this verse. It shows these are alternative options, not that all of them will happen together.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda shows the action is done with intensity or to multiple people
Adding yu- changes it from 'he crucified' to 'being crucified' - shows someone is receiving the action
Adding -ū at the end shows this is happening to multiple people
Basic connecting word that shows different possible punishments in this verse
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repetitive
Adding تُ at the beginning shows this is happening to something rather than something doing it
Forms the basic singular noun for 'hand'
Changes singular 'hand' into plural 'hands' using broken plural pattern
Completes the plural form by adding final ي

steps.m: their hands (object of تُقَطَّعَ - to be cut off)
steps.n: The word 'hands' gets 'their' added to it (هِمْ means 'their'). It's the thing getting cut off in this sentence.
Root letters put together with vowels to make the basic word for 'foot'
Changes the singular 'foot' to plural 'feet' by adding أ at start and changing vowels
Adds third person masculine plural possessive pronoun
Connects this word to أَيْدِيهِمْ (their hands) in the ayah as part of the list of things being cut off
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound
Added sukun at the end to show where the word stops

steps.m: to be opposite or different from something

steps.n: The ا makes it a noun meaning 'the act of opposing'
Added tanween kasra to show this word is being acted upon by مِنْ (from) in the phrase مِّنْ خِلٰفٍ (from opposite sides)
This is a connecting word that shows different possible punishments in this ayah - being killed OR crucified. It gives equal options between the connected parts.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to يُ at start and added ى at end to show someone is being banished (passive)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being banished
Basic preposition showing something coming from somewhere or indicating a starting point
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth). This shows the preposition is linked to what comes after it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together. The fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter make this a basic noun.
The prefix 'ال' makes the word definite, like saying 'the' in English. It shows we're talking about a specific earth.
The kasra ending shows this word follows the preposition فِى (in) in this ayah
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding لِكَ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something mentioned before (in this case, the punishment described in the previous ayah)
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ which refers to multiple people - in this case referring back to ٱلَّذِينَ (those who) from earlier in the verse
This is the basic concept of being disgraced or feeling shame
Changed from action to noun by adding kasra at start and sukun in middle. This makes it into the actual feeling or state of disgrace.

steps.m: a disgrace (in this ayah: their disgrace)
steps.n: Added tanween damma at the end to show this is a general concept of disgrace. In this ayah, it refers to the disgrace that will come to those who fight against Allah and His messenger.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it shows where the corruption/mischief is happening - in the earth/land.
Changed the pattern to make a feminine adjective meaning 'lower' or 'nearer', which came to refer to this world as the 'lower life'

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the hereafter)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific concept of 'this worldly life'. The word appears in contrast with 'the hereafter' (ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing that something (the disgrace mentioned) belongs to or is for someone
هم shows this refers to multiple males - specifically the wrongdoers mentioned earlier in the ayah who will receive disgrace

steps.m: and for them (connecting to previous punishments)
steps.n: و connects this phrase about disgrace to the previous punishments mentioned (being killed, crucified, etc.)
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the disgrace/humiliation happens within or during worldly life.
Changes root letters into a word meaning 'the one who comes after' by adding vowel marks
Adds long aa (ا) to make it mean 'the one who comes last/after'
Adds ة at the end to make it feminine and creates the special meaning 'afterlife' (the final/last life after this one)
Added definite article ال to specify this particular afterlife
Added kasra at end to show this word is connected to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is described by the adjective عَظِيمٌ (great/severe) which follows it.
Adding the double dammah makes this an indefinite noun - meaning 'a punishment' rather than 'the punishment'
Added ي between ظ and م with vowels to show this describes something as having this quality. This word describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah.
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) at the end to make the word indefinite, meaning 'a great punishment' rather than 'the great punishment'
This is a basic exception word that shows something is being left out from what was mentioned before. It's like saying 'but not' in English.

Adding the shadda and alif makes the word stronger and connects it to what comes after it. In this ayah, it's connecting to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) to show they are being excluded from something mentioned earlier.
Added the definite article ال with a shadda on the ل to make it specific. This word connects descriptions to people or things.

steps.m: those who (subject of تَابُوا - they repented)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is describing the people who repented (تَابُوا) in this ayah.

steps.m: to return back from wrong to right
he returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا in past tense.
Added وا to show that many people did this action. This word describes what الَّذِينَ (those who) did.
This is a preposition that shows where or when something starts. Here it connects the verb 'repented' (تَابُوا) with 'before' (قَبْلِ) to show when the repentance happened.

steps.m: to come before or in front of

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a word meaning 'before' in time or place
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to أَن that comes after it, creating the meaning 'before that...'

steps.m: that (introduces the clause تَقْدِرُوا۟ you gain power)
steps.n: This particle connects two ideas by introducing what happens. In this ayah, it connects the idea of 'before' with 'you gain power over them'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' by adding وا. The word relates to عَلَيْهِمْ (over them) in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to ي when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this affects 'them'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed from 'he knew' to 'know!' by adding 'i' at start and changing vowels to make it a command
Adds connective particle 'so/then' to link with previous statement

steps.m: so know! (command to all of you)
steps.n: Adding the masculine plural imperative suffix وا to indicate multiple addressees
This is a basic particle that connects parts of a sentence, like saying 'that' in English
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) and fatha makes the meaning stronger. This particle introduces the important statement that follows about Allah being forgiving.
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The Deity' - the one true God. The ال makes it specific and unique.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل and fatha at the end to show Allah is being described. The hamza at the start changed to a joining alif since it connects to the word before it.
Added و after second root letter and vowels to show someone who does a lot of forgiving
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing Allah's attribute of being Most Forgiving. This word pairs with رَّحِيمٌ (Most Merciful) to show two of Allah's qualities.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed to pattern showing someone who does something intensely
Added tanween dammah to make the word indefinite
The letter ر has a shaddah (doubled) to show it connects smoothly with the word before it

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: Adding damma to show it's in nominative case
Adds the calling particle يٰ with a long extension mark to show someone is being called from afar

steps.m: O you (calling attention to ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Adds ها to make the call more specific and draw attention to the believers being addressed
This is a special demonstrative pronoun used to refer to a group of people
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to اتَّقُوا (they feared) in this ayah as part of 'O you who believe, fear Allah...'
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed first letter to ا and added ت with shadda to show doing the action to yourself (like 'self-protect')
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Changed the word to the special form used only for Allah, with doubled ل to show uniqueness

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared in ٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being feared in the command 'fear Allah'. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added ٱ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action thoroughly for oneself
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and seek earnestly (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from 'he sought' to 'you all seek!' by adding وا at the end to make it a command for a group
This is a word that shows movement or direction towards something
Changed ى to ي with sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him'. This word connects to ٱبْتَغُوٓا۟ (seek) in the ayah, showing what to seek towards.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Changes from verb (seeking) to noun (thing used for seeking)
Adds ة at the end to make it a specific path or way to reach something

steps.m: the means, the way of approaching
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show increased effort or intensity
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word to previous commands in the verse
Added damma at end to show this is in nominative case
Added وا at the end to change from 'he strives' to 'you all strive' - making it a command for a group of people
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects with سَبِيلِهِۦ (His path) to show 'in His path'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to make a way or path

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added kasra at the end to show this path belongs to something or connects with another word
Added هِۦ to show the path belongs to Him (Allah)
This is a special word that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen. It's like saying 'hopefully' in English.

Added كُمْ at the end to show this hope applies to multiple people being spoken to. This connects to the next word تُفْلِحُونَ (succeed/prosper) to complete the meaning 'perhaps you all will succeed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Added تُ prefix for 'you' and changed vowels for present tense

steps.m: you all achieve success (addressing the believers)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This is addressing the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

This is a special word that emphasizes what comes after it. It makes the next word change its ending to show it's being emphasized. In this ayah, it's emphasizing the statement about those who disbelieve.
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because ذ is a sun letter. Added fatha at end because this word is the subject of كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved).

steps.m: to reject/deny truth, cover up, be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb. This word describes what the people mentioned in إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ (indeed those who) did.
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together. The small mark after وا (۟) is just to help with pronunciation.
This word introduces an imaginary situation that didn't happen. In this ayah, it talks about if the disbelievers had everything on earth (which they don't).

Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger. It connects the previous part 'if those who disbelieved had' to what comes after 'all that is in the earth'. The shadda shows what follows is being emphasized as true.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ to show this is about a male person or thing
Changed from singular (him) to plural (them) by adding م

steps.m: what (connects to لَوْ to mean 'if what')
steps.n: Added fatha to show it's being used as a connector word

steps.m: whatever (connects to لَوْ to mean 'if whatever')
steps.n: The shadda shows this word is strongly connected to the word before it (لَوْ) to create the meaning 'if whatever'
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show what is 'in' the earth.
Connected the letters with basic vowel marks to form the noun
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and ِ at end because it comes after the word فِى (in)

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Adding ي between letters and vowel marks changes this from an action to describing something that has this quality completely

steps.m: all together (describing how something in the ayah is done)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end makes this word describe how something happens. In this ayah, it's describing how much of what's on earth is being talked about - all of it together.

steps.m: to be similar or equal to something

steps.n: Changed the idea of being similar into a word for something that is similar
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and something similar or equal (as object)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: and its equal (referring to everything on earth mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this is equal to what was mentioned before (everything on earth)
Basic preposition meaning 'with'. This word has no root since it's a preposition.

steps.m: with him (referring to مِثْلَهُۥ - its like/equivalent)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being accompanied. The ۥ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel. This refers back to مِثْلَهُۥ (its equivalent) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to pay something to free someone/something

steps.n: Added اِ at start and ت in middle to show doing the action to/for yourself - like paying your own ransom
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Added وا suffix to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end drops because two vowel sounds can't come together

steps.m: so that they may pay to free themselves (connected to مَا تُقُبِّلَ - it would not be accepted)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show this is the purpose or reason for something. In this ayah, it's explaining what wouldn't be accepted from them.
Basic preposition showing how something is done or used

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - what is in the earth)
steps.n: Added هِ to show this refers back to what is in the earth that the disbelievers would want to ransom themselves with
Added ۦ which is just a pronunciation mark showing the هِ should be clearly pronounced
Basic preposition showing that something is being separated or distanced from punishment. In this ayah, it shows what the disbelievers are trying to save themselves from.

steps.m: to punish severely or cause pain

steps.n: Changed the root 'punish' into a noun 'punishment' by adding alif
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or describes يَوْمِ (day)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: of the day (part of عَذَابِ يَوْمِ - punishment of the day)
steps.n: The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else. Here it's connected to عَذَابِ (punishment) to mean 'punishment of the day'
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding fathas (َ) to make 'he stood up'
Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'standing' by adding ي
Added feminine ending to specify one great instance of rising
Added definite article to specify THE resurrection
Added kasra to show possession by the word يَوْمِ (day of)
This is a relative pronoun used here to mean 'whatever' or 'all that'. In this ayah, it refers to everything that exists on earth.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accept'
Added shadda to show intensity of action
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something being done to the subject
Added تُ at start to show something being done to the subject
Basic preposition showing separation or starting point. In this ayah, it's used with عَذَابِ (punishment) to show what they want to be saved from.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this refers back to ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ (those who disbelieved) mentioned at the start of the ayah
Basic preposition showing possession or purpose. Here it connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) to show who the punishment is for
Added هُمْ to show who receives something. Here it refers back to ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ (those who disbelieved) mentioned earlier

steps.m: and for them (connecting to previous statement about their ransom not being accepted)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this phrase to the previous statement, showing an additional consequence
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this verb into a noun that names the result of the action
Adding tanween dammah (ٌ) makes this an indefinite noun. This word is the subject that أَلِيمٌ (painful) describes.
Added vowels and ي to create an intensive adjective describing something that causes a lot of pain. This word describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) in this ayah.
Added double dammah to make the word indefinite - now means 'a painful (thing)'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form

steps.m: he wants/seeks (subject of يَخْرُجُوا - to exit)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. The middle letter و became ي to make it easier to say. Shows ongoing wanting/seeking.

steps.m: they want/seek (subject of يَخْرُجُوا - to exit)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action. This matches with يَخْرُجُوا (to exit) which is also plural.
This particle connects the verbs يُرِيدُونَ (they want) and يَخْرُجُوا (to exit), showing that exiting is what they want. It's like saying 'they want to exit' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to show ongoing action

steps.m: they exit/are exiting (trying to do this from النَّارِ/the Fire)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action. This word shows what the disbelievers want to do - exit from the Fire.
Basic preposition showing something coming from or out of something else. Here it connects to 'the Fire' (ٱلنَّارِ) to show what they want to get out from.
Added fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word that starts with ال (the). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'fire'
Adding shadda emphasizes the intensity of the fire
Adds the definite article ال to specify this particular fire (referring to the fire of Hell in this context)

steps.m: from/of the intense fire (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Starting with the basic negative particle ما which means 'not'

steps.m: and not (they are not from خٰرِجِينَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This وَمَا negates that they will be خٰرِجِينَ (those who exit) from the fire.
Basic pronoun form meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed gender)
Adding damma (ُ) shows this is the subject 'they' who are doing the action of wanting (يُرِيدُونَ) in this ayah
Added ا after first letter and vowel marks to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: able to leave/exit (in context of مَا هُم - they are not)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show ability or possibility, especially when used with negation (مَا هُم) to mean 'they are not able to'

steps.m: those who are able to leave/exit
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) to make it grammatically complete in the sentence
Basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from it/her (referring to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show this is happening to/from something feminine (in this case, the Fire mentioned earlier in the verse)
Combines the preposition ل (for/to) with the pronoun هم (them) to create the meaning 'for them'

steps.m: and for them (connected to عَذَابٌ - punishment)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This shows they will have both: not being able to leave the fire AND an enduring punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding alif after second root letter turns the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is being described by مُّقِيمٌ (lasting) in this ayah.
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite, meaning 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who/what does the action. The و changed to ي because of the kasra before it.
The م has a shadda because it comes after a word ending in ن or ل, which merges into the م
Added the two dots (tanween) to show this word describes the punishment mentioned
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (to steal) to a noun meaning the person who does the stealing
Adds definite article and doubles the س with shadda
Adds damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Adds conjunction 'and' at the beginning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun for someone who does the action
Added ة to make the word feminine, meaning a woman who steals
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س letter
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word

steps.m: and the female thief (subject of فَٱقْطَعُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the command that follows (cut off their hands)

steps.m: to cut off completely or sever

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he cut off' in the past tense
Changes from 'he cut' to 'cut!' by adding alif at start - this makes it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people

steps.m: so cut off! (command to a group)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic singular noun
Changed to broken plural form using pattern أَفْعِل to show multiple hands
Added a fatha sound at the end to connect with 'my' pronoun
Added هُمَا to show the hands belong to two people. This word is the object (thing being cut) in relation to the command 'cut' (فَٱقْطَعُوٓا۟)
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed verb into a noun by adding ء at the end
Added tanween to show this is the object explaining why the cutting should be done, connecting it to the command 'cut' (فَٱقْطَعُوٓا۟) in the verse
Starting with the basic relative pronoun that means 'what'
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause/reason. In this ayah, it connects to كَسَبَا (they both earned) to explain why the punishment happens.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he earned' in the past tense

steps.m: they both earned (referring to السَّارِقُ وَٱلسَّارِقَةُ - the male and female thief)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to show that two people (the male and female thief mentioned) are doing the action together
Added alif to make it a noun meaning the result of deterring - something that serves as a warning to prevent others from doing wrong
The ًا ending shows this word explains what kind of recompense (جَزَآءًۢ) the punishment serves as - it's meant to stop others from stealing
Added fatha at the end to connect this word with the next word (اللَّهِ)
Added shadda on the م because it comes after a word ending in ن or tanween, which makes the pronunciation smoother
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Adding 'al' makes it specific - like saying 'the god' instead of just 'a god'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being described by another word
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is a unique word.
Added shadda to emphasize the ل sound, which is a special feature of this word

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence. Allah is described as being عَزِيزٌ (Mighty) and حَكِيمٌ (Wise).

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from an action (becoming strong) to describing someone/something as mighty - like turning 'ran' into 'runner'
The tanween (double damma) shows this is a complete thought. This word describes Allah in this ayah, showing His power and might.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: one who is full of wisdom (description of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he judged' (a past action) to 'full of wisdom' (a lasting quality) by using a special pattern that shows Allah has complete wisdom always
The added dots (tanween) at the end show this is describing Allah as All-Wise
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to the person who repents (تَابَ)
The فَ prefix shows this is a result or consequence - in this case, introducing what happens to whoever repents

steps.m: to return back from wrong to right
he returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he' did the action

steps.m: he returned back (from wrong to right)
steps.n: Changed to hollow verb form by adding alif, which is common for verbs with و in middle
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects 'his wrongdoing' (ظُلْمِهِۦ) to the verb 'repented' (تَابَ).
Added kasra (ِ) under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) after the ن. This helps connect the word smoothly to the next word when reading.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word after it (ظُلْمِهِۦ meaning 'his wrongdoing')
Changed the action into a noun by adding damma and sukun. This makes it describe the act itself rather than doing it.
Added kasra to show this wrongdoing belongs to someone
Added هِۦ to show the wrongdoing belongs to him. This refers back to whoever is repenting in the ayah.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb
Added أ at start to show causing something to happen. Changed vowel pattern to match this form.

steps.m: and he reformed/made better (connected to تَابَ - repented)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with the previous word تَابَ (repented), showing these actions happened together
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes the word اللَّهَ (Allah).
The فَ shows this is a result of the previous statement about repenting. It connects to the previous part saying 'whoever repents' by showing what happens as a result.
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a unique name for The One God
The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) on the ل to make it stronger, starts with a connecting alif (ٱ), and has a small line on top at the end to show someone is talking about Allah
Added damma to show basic verb form
Adds يَ at start to make it 'he' doing the action in present tense

steps.m: he repents (subject of Allah's mercy in this ayah)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the main doer of the action. In this ayah, this word describes someone who repents, making them the recipient of Allah's mercy.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. It's like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English.
When adding a pronoun after this word, the ى changes to يْ. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to Allah يَتُوبُ - turns in mercy)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who or what is being referred to. Here it refers back to Allah who is mentioned in يَتُوبُ (turns in mercy).

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
steps.n: This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. Here it emphasizes that Allah is truly forgiving and merciful. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show this emphasis.
Changed from action to noun - something that is worshipped
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل with shadda to show this is the one true God. The fatha at the end shows Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to damma and added و after it to show this is a strong quality

steps.m: very forgiving (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added two dots (tanween) at the end to show this word stands alone as a complete description

steps.m: one who shows lots of kindness
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who does this action a lot
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)

steps.m: one who shows endless kindness (describing Allah)
steps.n: The letter ر has a shaddah (doubled) to connect smoothly with previous word, meaning stays similar
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' and makes them past tense
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'. In this ayah, it's asking 'Don't you know?' expecting the answer 'Yes, I do know.'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
The ت at the start shows you're doing the action. The sukun marks (ْ) at the end show this is being used in a special way with أَلَمْ (do you not)

steps.m: that (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (double mark) on ن makes the word stronger. This shows what comes after is a definite fact. Here it connects 'Did you not know' with the fact that 'Allah has dominion over the heavens and earth'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

This is the unique name for God in Islam, formed by adding ال (the) and emphasizing the ل with a shadda. The أ changes to ٱ at the start. The َ at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ with an elongation mark (ۥ) to show this means 'him/it'. This word shows that something belongs to Allah in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun by adding damma and sukun. This pattern creates a noun meaning the result or domain of ruling.
The ُ ending shows this is the subject of لَهُۥ (belongs to Him). In this ayah, it's stating that the kingdom of the heavens and earth belongs to Allah.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
The verb 'to rise up' became the noun 'sky' by adding hamza at the end
Added وات at the end to make it mean 'many skies' instead of one sky
Added 'the' and doubled the س to show it's definite
Added kasra at end to show this belongs to Allah in this ayah
Basic root letters got vowel marks to make the word 'earth'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something else in the sentence - in this case it's connected to مُلْكُ (dominion/ownership of) from earlier in the ayah

steps.m: the earth/land/ground (the specific one everyone knows)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to THE earth - the planet we live on - not just any land
Conjunction و adds meaning of 'and'

steps.m: to punish, cause pain or suffering

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'he punished' - root letters given vowels to make it a complete word

Added fatha vowels and the middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Changed from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants) by adding يَ at the start
Added long alif to show the extended nature of wanting/wishing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding ي at the start changes it from past to present tense
Added و to connect this with يُعَذِّبُ (punishes) showing Allah does both actions
This is a special word (relative pronoun) that doesn't come from a root. It asks about or refers to people in a general way.
Added لِ at the beginning to show this word is connected to يَغْفِرُ (He forgives). It shows who receives the forgiveness.
Added fatha vowels and the weak letter ا replaced ي due to Arabic spelling rules
Adding يَ at the start shows this is happening now or in the future. The ُ at the end shows this is the doer form.
Adding ا makes the middle of the word longer when you say it

steps.m: Allah (the proper name of God in Arabic)
steps.n: This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Arabic.

steps.m: Allah (the proper name of God in Arabic)

steps.m: Allah (as the subject doing the action)

steps.m: and Allah (connecting to the previous statement)

Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a common preposition

steps.m: between them (referring to the people mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. This connects to the previous mentions of people in the ayah.

steps.m: or (connecting two choices: judge between them OR turn away from them)
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word (like 'or' in English) that gives a choice between two options. In this verse, it connects two possible actions: judging between them or turning away from them.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding أ at start and changing vowels
This is a preposition that shows separation or relation

steps.m: away from them (referring to the people mentioned in أَعْرِضْ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being turned away from. This connects to the command أَعْرِضْ (turn away) in the previous part of the verse.
Joining of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)

steps.m: to turn away or present something

Added تُ to make it a present tense verb addressing 'you'
This is a preposition that shows moving away from or turning aside from something

steps.m: away from them (referring to those mentioned in أَكَّالُونَ لِلسُّحْتِ - the devourers of the unlawful)

Added فَ (so/then) at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before

Added و at the end to change from 'he' to 'they' - like adding 's' in English

steps.n: Added كَ to show who is being harmed

Connected the letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun. The hamza at the end is written above the line.

steps.n: Added tanween and alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it shows that 'they will not harm you (in) any way'.
Shows a thing being acted on by adding an alif at the end
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you judge'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about judging between them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed the final vowel to a sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the ending
Added تَ at the end to show that 'you' did the action in the past
Basic meaning of judging
Changed the root letters into a command by adding the 'u' vowel in the middle and making it silent at the end
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier

steps.m: so judge! (command in response to what came before)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result of what was mentioned before. The final sukun is dropped because it's not needed at the end of the word.
Added fatha and sukun to form the preposition 'between'

steps.m: between them (refers to judging between the people mentioned in فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'them' to show who is being separated or what is between. This refers back to the people that Muhammad ﷺ is being asked to judge between in the ayah.
Forms the noun 'justice' from root meaning fairness
Root letters got vowels to make the noun meaning 'justice' - like turning 'fair' into 'fairness'
Added the definite article 'the'
Added 'with' at the beginning and the proper ending vowel to show its role in the sentence
Adding the shadda and kasra changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'. This word emphasizes the importance of what follows (اللَّهَ - Allah).
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and a shadda

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُحِبُّ - He loves)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being loved in this sentence. Also changed the initial alif to a wasla since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda

steps.m: he loves (Allah loves the just ones)
steps.n: Added يُ at the start to show it's happening now (present), changed middle vowel to ِ, and added ُ at the end to show Allah is doing the loving

steps.m: one who acts justly (object of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: The مُ prefix and ِ vowel work together to show who does the action. This word is what Allah loves (يُحِبُّ) in this ayah.

steps.m: those who act justly (object of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: Forms regular masculine plural

steps.m: the ones who act justly (object of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' specific just people

steps.m: the ones who act justly (whom Allah loves)
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of Allah's love (يُحِبُّ)
This is a question word that always asks about the way something happens
Adding the basic vowels to make the word readable - fatha on kaf, sukun on ya

steps.m: and how (asking about how they seek your judgment)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this question with the previous statement. This word is asking about how they seek the Prophet's judgment when they have Allah's law with them.

steps.m: to make someone judge/appoint as judge
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
Adding يُ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now/in future (instead of past)
Adding ون shows multiple people (they) are doing the action
