Changed the ending to ى which is common for this type of verb
Added vowels and ي to make it describe someone or something that has this quality
Added ُ to show this is the subject being talked about
Added ٱلْ to point to a specific bad thing/person. This word is being contrasted with ٱلطَّيِّبُ (the good/pure one) in this ayah.
Added vowels and shadda to make it an adjective. The shadda makes the meaning stronger.
Added the definite article ال. The first letter gets a shadda because it comes after ال.
Added the ُ ending to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect it to the previous word. The alif in ال changes to ٱ after و.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - fatha on ل and sukun on و
Added و with fatha at the beginning. When و combines with لو, it changes the meaning from just 'if' to 'even if'. This word connects to أَعْجَبَكَ (amazes you) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and changing the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) makes it about causing amazement in someone else
Adding ك at the end means 'you' is receiving the amazement - like saying 'amazed YOU' instead of just 'amazed'
Changed from a verb (doing) to a noun (thing) by adding vowel marks - now means 'a lot of something'
Added ة to make it into a specific instance of abundance
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the amazing in the phrase 'even if its abundance amazes you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (doing evil) to a word meaning something/someone that is very evil
Adding the definite article makes it specific
Adding kasra for genitive case

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The ت merges with ق creating a doubled sound (shadda)
Changed to a command form for multiple people by adding وا at the end

steps.m: so protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added a small alif above to make the 'aa' sound longer
Added 'the' to make it definite
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
This is the base word meaning 'possessors of' or 'people of'. It's used before another word to show ownership or association. Here it connects to الْأَلْبٰبِ (understanding/intellect).
Added يٰٓ at the beginning to call out to these people, like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!' in English. The long alif (ٰ) and hamza (ٓ) make the call stronger.
Changes from verb to noun meaning the core/essence
Changes from single 'core/mind' (لُبّ) to plural form (أَلْبَاب) to mean multiple minds
Adds the definite article 'the'
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to يٰٓأُو۟لِى (O people of)
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. لَعَلَّ is a particle that shows hope or expectation for something to happen.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to تُفْلِحُونَ (you will succeed) in the next ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense (fatha sounds) to present tense (kasra sound) - like changing 'succeeded' to 'succeeds'
Added تُ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the action

steps.m: to point to or indicate something
which/who (asking about or pointing to something)
steps.n: The root letters combine with a fatha and doubled middle letter to make a question word
Added the calling particle يٰٓ at the beginning and damma at the end to show we're addressing someone
Added ها to make it address a group of people
This is the basic form of the word used to describe or point to someone or something
Changed from describing one thing to describing many things
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the ones' instead of just 'ones'
Added final pronunciation marks and connected it to the next word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed/had faith). Together they mean 'those who believed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to having complete faith

steps.m: they believed (referring to believers being addressed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word refers back to 'O you who believe' (يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ) at the start of the verse.
This is a negative particle that comes before verbs to negate them. Here it negates تَسْـَٔلُوا۟ (ask), telling people not to ask.
Added fatha mark (َ) to help with pronunciation
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed middle vowels to show present tense
Added وا at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action
This is a preposition that connects words by showing what something is about. Here it connects 'do not ask' (تَسْـَٔلُوا۟) with 'things' (أَشْيَآءَ), meaning 'do not ask about things'.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters were given vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the common noun meaning 'thing'
Changes from single 'thing' to broken plural 'things' - like how 'foot' becomes 'feet' in English
Final fatha indicates accusative case for indefinite noun

steps.m: if (introduces condition for تُبْدَ - it will be shown)
steps.n: This is a basic word that shows a condition - if something happens, then something else will happen. Here it means 'if these things are shown to you, they will upset you.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: it is shown/revealed (passive verb in تُبْدَ لَكُمْ - it will be shown to you)
steps.n: Changes to passive voice in present tense. The تُ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future. The sukun (ْ) and final vowel change show it's affected by another word.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (object of تُبْدَ - it will be made clear)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كم with damma and sukun to show it means 'you all'. This word is receiving the action of تُبْدَ (it will be made clear) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happens in the present

steps.m: it makes you all sad (referring to the questions mentioned in أَشْيَآءَ)
steps.n: The word changed to show who is being made sad - 'you all' (كم) was added at the end
Adds coordinating conjunction و (and) to the conditional particle إِن (if)
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Added تَ at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense and second person (you)

steps.m: you (all) ask about (negative command with لَا)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show this refers to a group. The لَا before this word makes it a command not to do something.
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or concerning
The ها suffix refers back to أَشْيَآءَ (things) that were mentioned earlier. This word connects to تَسْـَٔلُوا (you ask) to show what is being asked about.
Adding the kasra (ِ) vowel mark makes this a noun meaning 'time'

steps.m: when, at the time (of يُنَزَّلُ - it is being sent down)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing when the Quran is being sent down
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action happens multiple times or with extra force

steps.m: it is being sent down repeatedly (the Quran)
steps.n: Adding يُ at the start and ُ at the end changes this to a present tense passive verb, showing the action is happening to something
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'that which is recited'. The ان ending makes it stronger and more complete.
Adds the definite article ال to specify this is THE recitation (The Quran), and adds ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
The root letters were joined together with short 'a' sounds (fatha), and the last letter و changed to ا to show something happened in the past
Changed to passive present tense by adding تُ at start and changing vowels. The ى at the end is because it's a weak verb.

steps.m: it is made clear/apparent (passive voice, conditional)
steps.n: The ى at the end is removed because it comes after 'if' (إِن), making it a conditional 'what if' statement
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on ل

steps.m: for you all (being shown to in تُبْدَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all'. This word shows who is being shown things in the verb تُبْدَ (they are shown/revealed).
Added fathas to make it past tense. The و will change in the next step due to being a weak letter.
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of the word
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific/definite
This is a preposition that shows something is being moved away from, asked about, or discussed. In this ayah, it's used with asking (تَسْـَٔلُوا) about something.

steps.m: about it/concerning it (referring to أَشْيَآءَ - things)
steps.n: The هَا refers back to أَشْيَآءَ (things) mentioned earlier in the ayah that people are being told not to ask about.
This is a unique word that comes from the root meaning of divinity but has become the proper name of God in Arabic. The double ل with shadda makes it special.
Added fatha on the shadda to show correct pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is doing the action in this sentence - specifically the pardoning mentioned in عَفَا (He pardoned)

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of عَفَا/pardoned)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, showing this is an additional quality of Allah being described
This is the basic concept of forgiving or pardoning someone
Added vowels and و to show this describes someone who forgives very much. This word describes Allah in this ayah.
Added tanween to show this is indefinite, meaning it's describing one of Allah's many attributes

steps.m: one who is extremely patient and forbearing (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Forms an emphatic adjective showing Allah's perfect and complete patience/forbearance

steps.m: Most Patient, Most Forbearing (divine attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The double dots (tanwin) at the end show this is describing Allah as perfectly patient and forbearing
This is a special word that adds certainty when talking about past actions. In this ayah, it emphasizes that some people definitely asked these questions before.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he asked her/it (referring to a question mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what was asked about. In this ayah, it refers back to the questions being discussed.

steps.m: to stand firmly, establish, rise up

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand' becomes 'people' - a group that stands together. In this ayah, these people are the ones doing the asking (سَأَلَهَا).
Added double-damma to show this is an undefined subject (like saying 'a people' instead of 'the people')
This is a basic word that shows something comes from, is part of, or is among other things. In this ayah, it connects قَبْلِ (before) with كُمْ (you all).
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a voweled letter in speech, making the م sound stronger. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha and sukun to create the noun meaning 'before'
The kasra shows this word is being possessed by something that comes after it
Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed (you all/your)
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time in between. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَصْبَحُوا۟ (they became) to show what happened next.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: to become, to enter the morning
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning and changed first vowel to sukun to make it mean 'became'
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to كٰفِرِينَ (disbelievers) to mean 'they became disbelievers'
This is a basic preposition showing how something happens. Here it connects to قَوْمٌ (people) to show what they did something with.

steps.m: with it/her (referring to the questions mentioned earlier)
steps.n: The ها refers back to the questions (أسئلة, feminine plural) that were asked, which were mentioned earlier in the ayah with سَأَلَهَا (asked them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to describe someone who does that action

steps.m: rejecters (as objects of أَصْبَحُوا - they became)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this is plural (many rejecters) and that they are receiving the action of the verb أَصْبَحُوا (they became)
This is a negative particle that comes before verbs to show that something did not happen. Here it negates the verb جَعَلَ (made/established) to say 'Allah did not establish...'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is part of the phrase 'Allah did not make/establish' (ما جَعَلَ اللَّهُ) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of جَعَلَ - made/ordained)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and merged it with the word. The shadda shows emphasis. This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. This word is the subject (doer) of جَعَلَ (ordained/made) in this ayah.

This is a basic preposition that connects two parts of the sentence, showing that Allah did not make/establish FROM these things. It links the verb جَعَلَ (made/established) with بَحِيرَةٍ (Bahirah - a type of sacred animal).
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show this is a quality or state

steps.m: a she-camel that is split (in ears)
steps.n: Added ة to make it feminine and specify one instance of something with this quality

steps.m: a she-camel whose ears have been split (a pre-Islamic practice)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any such she-camel)
This is the basic negation word that says 'no' or 'not' to something
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous word بَحِيرَةٍ, showing another thing that doesn't exist

steps.m: to let loose or roam freely
steps.n: This pattern creates an active participle meaning 'one who/which is loose'
Added vowels and alif to create active participle pattern. This shows who or what does the action.
Added feminine marker ة to specify this refers to a female animal

steps.m: a she-camel set free for idols (object of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added genitive case ending ٍ. This word is the object of جَعَلَ (made/designated) in this verse, referring to a pre-Islamic practice where pagans would set certain camels free in honor of their idols.
Basic negation particle that makes something negative. Here it negates سَآئِبَةٍ (a loose she-camel), saying 'not a loose she-camel'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous one, like saying 'not this AND not that'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show a quality or characteristic

steps.m: female camel that is joined (to another)
steps.n: Added ة to make it feminine and specify it refers to a female camel

steps.m: a female camel that is joined (to another)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show it's indefinite and in the genitive case
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous one, making a list of things that Allah did not establish
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى when it has a fatha.

Changed to active participle form (doer of action). The ي changes to tanwin kasra (ٍ) because it's indefinite and acted upon. This word is what Allah did not establish/make, as shown by جَعَلَ (made/established).
This is the basic form of the word that means 'but'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above it.
Added shadda and fatha at the end to add emphasis, like saying 'but indeed' or 'but truly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement to what came before it
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ٱلَّ makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ينَ makes it refer to many people. This word is connected to يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent) in this ayah, showing who is doing the inventing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense singular form
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did this action in the past

steps.m: to make something up or create falsely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ٱ and ت changes it to mean doing the action to/for oneself
Added يَ at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, they are making up lies specifically against Allah.

This is a preposition that shows the direction of the lying/inventing mentioned in يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent) towards Allah. In this context it means 'against' rather than 'upon' because it's describing false things being attributed against Allah.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of يَفْتَرُونَ (they fabricate). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects with previous word.
Adding vowel marks to show this is a complete past tense verb meaning 'he lied'
Added the definite article 'the' at the beginning. This word is what is being fabricated in the phrase 'they fabricate the lie'
Added vowels to make it mean 'became many/numerous' in the past tense
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمْ to show this refers to 'them'
Added وَ at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَعْقِلُونَ (they understand), making it mean 'they do not understand'
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: to use one's mind to understand

steps.n: Connected the letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past

steps.m: they understand (referring to those who disbelieve in يَفْتَرُونَ)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to those mentioned in يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent lies)

steps.m: when (introduces the condition قِيلَ 'it is said')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it sets up the condition 'when it is said to them...'

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition قِيلَ 'it is said')
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what came before it in the larger discussion
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was said (to لَهُمْ - to them)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that something was done to someone instead of someone doing it. This connects to لَهُمْ (to them) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb قِيلَ (it was said)
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group of males). This word shows who was being spoken to in قِيلَ (it was said)

steps.m: to rise up or be high

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the beginning to make it a command meaning 'come here' (literally: rise up towards me)
Added وْا at the end to make the command apply to many people instead of just one
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to 'the messenger' (ٱلرَّسُولِ) to show movement or direction towards him.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what/that which'. Here it connects to 'Allah revealed' (أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ) to mean 'what Allah revealed'. The elongated alif (آ) is just a writing variation of regular alif (ا) - it doesn't change the meaning.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a-a

steps.m: he caused to descend/sent down (subject of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action causative - showing that someone made something else descend. Here Allah (اللَّهُ) is the one doing the sending down.
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: The One True God (subject of أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Changed regular word for 'god' into the special name for The One True God by adding Al- at the start. Added ُ at the end to show Allah is doing the action.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it points to 'the messenger' (ٱلرَّسُولِ) as the destination.
Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to what came before it. This connects 'what Allah has revealed' with 'the messenger' in the complete phrase.
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added ال (the) at the beginning. The ل merges with ر making it stronger (doubled)
The ِ ending shows this comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to)
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Weak letter change - و changes to ا
Added وا at the end to show a group did the action

steps.m: to count, calculate, or consider something enough

steps.n: Changes verb into a noun meaning 'enough' or 'sufficient'
Added damma to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: sufficient for us (as subject of قَالُوا)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is sufficient for "us". This is what they said in response to being called to Allah's revelation.

steps.m: what/that which (refers to ما أنزل الله - what Allah revealed)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces what Allah revealed, connecting تَعَالَوْا (come) to أَنزَلَ (revealed).

steps.m: to find or come across something

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he found' in the past tense
Changed the last vowel to sukun to prepare for adding suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Basic preposition indicating position or status. This word has no root since it's a particle.
Added ي with sukun as a connecting letter to prepare for the pronoun suffix
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

steps.n: Root letters combine to make the simple word for 'father'
Changes the word to show many fathers
Adds 'our' to show these are our fathers
Starting with the question particle that shows disbelief or surprise
Adding و (wa) makes the question more emphatic, like saying 'is it REALLY that...?'
Adding لَوْ (if) to create a conditional expression that shows disapproval of what follows
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word describes what their fathers were like in the ayah.
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father

steps.n: Changes the singular word 'father' into a broken plural 'fathers' by adding alif and changing the pattern
Combines the two alifs into one long alif with madda
Adds damma to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was)
Adds هُمْ to show these are their fathers, referring back to those who were told to come to what Allah revealed
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in this ayah, making it mean 'they do not know'

steps.m: to know, understand, or be aware of

steps.n: Root letters got fatha marks to make 'he knew' in the past tense
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they know/understand (referring to those who follow their forefathers blindly)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who blindly follow their ancestors' ways without thinking.
Adding vowels to make the word pronounceable
Added tanween (two small lines) at the end to show this is the thing being acted upon. Makes it mean 'anything' instead of just 'thing'
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in this ayah
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This shows that their ancestors both 'did not know anything' AND 'were not guided'.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show receiving guidance rather than giving it
Changed اِ to يَ at start to show present tense
Added ون at end to show multiple people
Root letters combined with shadda (double letter) to make question word meaning 'which'
The vocative particle يا is added to call attention
Adding damma to indicate nominative case
Adding ها makes this a complete vocative phrase
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because it comes after ٱ.
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) which comes right after it.
Adding alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: they believed (subject of يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ - O you who)
steps.n: The suffix وا shows that many people did this action together. This word describes the people being addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ (O you who)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
When adding a suffix, على changes to عَلَيْ. The ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect with the next part.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This is like adding 'you all' in English.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, or be a self

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Changes to broken plural pattern by adding أَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: your selves (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show the word refers to multiple people being addressed
This is a negative particle that makes something negative. In this ayah, it negates يَضُرُّكُم (harm you), saying that something will not harm you.
Added fatha mark on ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense

steps.m: he harms you all (لَا negates this harm)
steps.n: Added كُم at end to show who is being harmed. This word is negated by لَا before it in the ayah, so it means 'will not harm you'
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م to emphasize the word. This word connects to ضَلَّ (went astray) in this ayah, showing who did the action.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone already did this action.

steps.m: he went completely astray (subject of يَضُرُّكُم)
steps.n: The doubled ل (with shadda) shows this wasn't just a small mistake - it means someone went completely off the right path. This word is connected to يَضُرُّكُم (harm you) in the ayah, showing who won't harm the believers if they stay guided.
This is a basic time particle that shows when something happens
Adding ا at the end makes it conditional, connecting it to اهْتَدَيْتُمْ (you are guided) to show 'when/if you are guided'

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and تَ after first letter to show someone finding guidance for themselves rather than guiding others
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding endings
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past
This is a basic preposition showing direction. In this ayah, it connects 'your return' (مَرْجِعُكُمْ) with 'Allah' (اللَّهِ) to show that the return is to Allah.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to or connects with something else (in this case, showing that the return mentioned in مَرْجِعُكُمْ belongs to Allah). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added مَ at the beginning to show where or when something happens, like changing 'return' to 'place of return'
Added كُمْ at the end to show it belongs to multiple people being spoken to. The ُ before كم helps connect the sounds. This word relates to returning to Allah mentioned earlier in إِلَى ٱللَّهِ.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Forms the adjective meaning 'all/entire' from the root meaning 'to gather'
Adds -an ending to show it describes HOW something is done (like 'completely' or 'all together')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - like telling something thoroughly or completely
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The أ changed to ئ because it comes after kasra.
Added فَ to show this happens after something else

steps.m: then he will inform you all thoroughly
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the information - it's all of you
This is a question word that asks about things or can refer to whatever/what
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with' or 'about'. This connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you all used to do) to mean 'about what you used to do'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The word changes from 'he was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle و and adding marks above and below letters

steps.m: you all were (past tense, referring to the believers addressed in يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people in the past tense
Root letters got vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make 'he worked' in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to point to or draw attention to something

steps.n: The damma (ُ) shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Adds يا ('ya') at start to make it a way of calling out to someone
Adds ها to make it refer to a group being addressed
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Changed the ending to make it refer to someone or something being described
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Added ين to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) to mean 'those who believed'
Added vowel marks and changed the style of writing ال to match Quranic spelling
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adding alif after first letter changes meaning to actively choosing to trust/believe

steps.m: they believed/had faith (referring to believers being addressed)
steps.n: Adding وا changes subject from 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)

steps.m: to witness something or tell what you saw

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Changed the verb into a noun by adding alif and ة, like changing 'testify' to 'testimony' in English
Changed the alif to an alif khanjariya (dagger alif) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: the testimony (subject of what comes after)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the main subject of what follows

steps.m: to be separate from or between

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added كُمْ which means 'you all' with ِ before it because بَيْن is a preposition (shows relationship between things). This word is part of شَهٰدَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ meaning 'testimony between you all'

steps.m: when (introduces the condition of حَضَرَ/death approaching)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a condition about death approaching. It connects the first part of the verse about testimony with when it should happen.

steps.m: to come near, to be present

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense. In this ayah, this verb is describing death (الموت) approaching someone.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb حَضَرَ (approached/came to)

steps.m: one of you (being acted upon by حَضَرَ)
steps.n: Attached pronoun indicates possession - 'of you (plural)'
Changed the action of dying into the noun 'death'. This word is what's doing the approaching in the phrase 'death approached'.
Added 'the' to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action of approaching
Adding the kasra (ِ) vowel mark makes this a noun meaning 'time'
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object

steps.m: to instruct, direct, or leave instructions

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a word meaning someone who instructs
Changed from 'one who instructs' to 'the instruction itself' by adding ة, making it a specific command or testament
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific testament

steps.m: the testament (possessed by or connected to something else)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: to bend, fold, or make into two parts

steps.n: Root meaning 'two' gets alif and sukun to make the basic number word 'two'
Added ان ending to make it a noun meaning 'two'
Added a small line (kasra) under the last letter to show it's connected to 'testimony' - like saying 'two witnesses'
This is a special word used to show possession. It doesn't come from a root but is a built-in word in Arabic.
Adding ا at the end makes this word refer to exactly two people or things. Here it describes two people of justice who will be witnesses.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes the testimony mentioned earlier in the verse
Added shadda on م to show emphasis and connection with previous word
Added pronoun كم meaning 'you all' to show who is being referred to (the believers being addressed in this ayah)

steps.m: or (connects two options: witnesses from among you OR from others)
steps.n: Basic coordinating conjunction that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects two possible sources of witnesses: either from among the believers or from non-believers when traveling.
Added alif and vowel marks to create the basic word for 'other'

steps.m: two others (referring to two witnesses from outside the community)
steps.n: Added انِ at the end to show this refers to exactly two others. In this ayah, it refers to two witnesses who are not from your own community.
Basic preposition showing that the two witnesses should be from among you. Here it connects 'two' (ٱثْنَانِ) with 'you all' (كُمْ) to mean 'two from among you'.
Added sukun (ْ) at the end to show the word stops here

steps.m: to be different from, to alter

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'other than' from the root meaning 'to be different from'

steps.m: other than you all (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Adds 'your (plural)' meaning by attaching كُمْ. This refers to the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) being addressed in this verse.

steps.m: if (introduces the condition for ضَرَبْتُمْ - you traveled)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects two parts: the condition (if you traveled) and what happens as a result (you hold them after prayer).
Basic 'you' pronoun form
Added ت with damma to show it's masculine 'you'
Added م with sukun to show it means multiple people

steps.m: to travel, journey, move about (or hit/strike)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed subject from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding م with sukun. This word connects to الْأَرْضِ (the land) in the ayah to show where the traveling happened.
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it shows where the traveling (ضَرَبْتُمْ) takes place.
This pattern makes the root into a noun meaning the physical earth/land
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific. The hamza on the alif has a special shape (ٱ) because it comes after the definite article.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word comes after the preposition فِى (in/on) in the ayah. Words after prepositions get this ending.

steps.m: to strike or hit a target
steps.n: Basic meaning of hitting or reaching something
Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'to cause something to happen'. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Adding ت at the end shows a feminine subject (she/it) did the action in the past
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happened after something else

steps.m: and then it afflicted you all (referring to مُّصِيبَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ - the affliction of death)
steps.n: Adding كُم at the end shows who was afflicted (you all). The ا changes to ٰ because it's followed by two consonants.

steps.m: to reach or hit a target
steps.n: Root meaning of hitting or striking
Added مُ at start and changed middle letter to ي to show something that hits/reaches its target
Added ة to make it feminine and specify one instance
The م gets doubled (shadda) and the word gets a ُ ending to show it's doing the action in the sentence
Basic concept of death
Adding the vowel mark changes this from a concept into a noun meaning 'death'
The dot (sukun) above و shows it's a still/quiet letter
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the death' instead of just 'death'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb حَضَرَ (approached/came to)
Basic meaning of restraining or holding back
Basic past tense verb form - added vowels to make 'he held back'
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: you all hold back both of them
steps.n: Added هُمَا to show who is being held back (both of them)
Basic preposition showing that the 'two others' mentioned are coming from or belonging to 'other than you'
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Root meaning of distance or being far
Basic noun form meaning 'after' or 'afterward'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word الصَّلَوٰةِ (the prayer) that comes after it
Adding basic vowels to make it pronounceable
Added a shadda (double letter) and long alif to make it a special noun meaning 'prayer' instead of the verb 'to pray'
Added feminine ending to specify one instance of prayer
Added 'the' to specify this particular prayer
Added kasra because it comes after the words مِنۢ بَعْدِ (after)
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing internal vowels
Added انِ to show exactly two people are doing the action

steps.m: and then they both swear an oath (after being detained)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of the previous action (being detained after prayer)
This is the base word for God in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.

steps.m: by/with Allah (as the one they swear by in يُقْسِمَانِ - they both swear)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show they are swearing by Allah's name. The ٱ is added as a helper letter for pronunciation.

steps.m: by/with Allah (as the one they swear by in يُقْسِمَانِ - they both swear)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُقْسِمَانِ (they both swear).
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects two parts: 'if you doubt' (إِنِ ٱرْتَبْتُمْ)
The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because it comes before a word starting with a hamza (ٱ)
Added reflexive form pattern to show doing the action to oneself
Weak letter change to make pronunciation easier
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past

This is a negative particle that negates the verb نَشْتَرِى (we buy/trade) that comes after it. In this ayah, it's being used to make an oath that they will not trade/exchange Allah's testimony for any price.
Adding ت between first and second root letters shows doing the action for oneself
Adding first person plural prefix to create present tense form
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added the pronoun 'hi' to show what the preposition connects to
Added a small alif (ۦ) after the pronoun. This is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to set a price or value on something

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to price) into a noun (a price) and added tanween to show it's the object being bought
This is a conditional particle used for hypothetical situations that didn't happen

steps.m: and even if (hypothetical condition in وَلَا نَكْتُمُ)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what comes after it in وَلَا نَكْتُمُ (and we do not conceal)
Added fathas (small diagonal lines) to make it mean 'he was' in the past. The middle fatha shows a 'he' did the action.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic for weak letters.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to something

steps.m: this/that (referring to something of close proximity)
steps.n: Added fatha and alif to make it point to something masculine and nearby. In this ayah, it refers to a relative (قُرْبَىٰ) mentioned right after it.
Changed vowels to make a noun meaning the concept of being near
Added ىٰ at the end to show this describes a specific type of closeness - being related by family
This is a negative particle that negates the verb نَكْتُمُ (we hide/conceal) that comes after it
The و connects this negation to the previous statement 'we do not sell it for any price', making this part mean 'and we do not hide...'
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and make the past tense form

steps.m: we hide/conceal (شَهٰدَةَ ٱللَّهِ - the testimony of Allah)
steps.n: Changed to present tense 'we' form. In this ayah, it refers to concealing Allah's testimony.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning the action itself
Added ة at the end to make it into a specific instance of witnessing
1. The alif is written as ٰ above the letter. 2. Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb نَكْتُمُ (we conceal)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added kasra at end to show 'of/belonging to'
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what comes after it, like saying 'I promise' or 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed we (the speakers in يُقْسِمَانِ - they both swear)
steps.n: Added آ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, this refers to the two witnesses who are swearing an oath.
This is a basic particle that refers to a specific time or situation

steps.m: in that case/therefore (connects to إِنَّآ - indeed we)
steps.n: Adding ًا changes this from just referring to time to showing a result or consequence. Here it connects to the statement 'indeed we' (إِنَّآ), showing the consequence of what was mentioned before.

steps.m: from (connecting ٱلْءَاثِمِينَ to the previous statement)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing that someone is from among a group. Here it connects to ٱلْءَاثِمِينَ (the sinners) in the ayah

steps.m: surely/indeed from (connecting ٱلْءَاثِمِينَ to the previous statement)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis to show certainty
The shadda (ّ) makes the ل sound stronger to add more emphasis, like saying 'most definitely' instead of just 'definitely'
A small 'a' sound (fatḥa) was added at the end to connect it to the next word
Changes verb into noun for someone who does the action. The alif is lengthened to آ because it comes at the start of the word.
Adds sound masculine plural ending to indicate multiple sinners
Adds ال to make it definite ('the'). This word comes after مِن in لَّمِنَ ('surely among') which explains why it ends in ينَ.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition - something that might happen

steps.m: then if (connecting to what comes after عُثِرَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what comes before it. It's like saying 'so if' or 'then if' in English.

steps.m: it was discovered (subject of إِنْ - if)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show something was discovered rather than someone doing the discovering. This word follows إِنْ (if) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. It comes from the idea of being high/elevated.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound more clearly. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) to show this word connects to the next word أَنَّهُمَا (that those two). Together they mean 'upon those two'.
This is an emphatic particle that introduces a statement with certainty
Added the dual pronoun هُمَا (those two) to show who the statement is about. This refers to the two people who are the subject of ٱسْتَحَقَّآ (they both deserved/committed)
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to make it past tense 'he was'
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the meaning of the root
Added ا at the end to show two people did the action
Added long alif at the end to show this refers to two people doing the action

steps.m: to do wrong, to commit sin

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This changes it from an action (doing wrong) to a thing (wrongdoing/sin).
Added tanween fatHa (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon in relation to the verb اسْتَحَقَّا (they both earned/deserved)

steps.m: to be behind or after something

steps.n: Added فَ (meaning 'then/so') at the start + changed root into 'another' form to mean 'then another one'

steps.m: then two others (who will take their place)
steps.n: Added انِ to show we're talking about exactly two others. This refers to the two new witnesses who will replace the previous ones.
Added يَ (ya) at start to make it mean 'he' in present tense, middle letter و (waw) makes long 'oo' sound

steps.m: they both stand/rise up (present, referring to the two others who will take their place)
steps.n: Added انِ suffix to show exactly two people doing the action. This refers to the two new witnesses mentioned in the ayah who will take the place of the original ones.
The و in the middle changed to ا to make 'he stood' in the past tense - this is a common pattern for words with و in the middle
Forms a noun indicating the place where standing occurs
Adds fatha to show this is the object of a verb
Adds 'his' to show ownership
Changes 'his' to 'their (two people)'

steps.m: from/of (connecting الَّذِينَ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) to what was mentioned before.

steps.m: from/of (connecting الَّذِينَ to what comes before)
steps.n: Added a fatha (َ) at the end because it comes before the definite article ال in الَّذِينَ. This helps make the pronunciation smoother.
Added ي to create the basic relative pronoun that connects descriptions to nouns
Added ال with shadda to make it specific

steps.m: those who (referring to the two witnesses mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Changed from 'the one who' to 'those who' by adding ين at the end - this makes it talk about multiple people instead of just one person
Added vowels to make it 'he deserved' in the past tense
Combined identical letters with shadda
Added است at beginning to show seeking or becoming entitled to something. This word relates to إِثْمًا (sin) which comes after it in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing position or obligation. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching pronouns. This is just a spelling change, not a meaning change.
Added هِمُ to show this affects multiple people. This word connects to يَقُومَانِ (they both stand) in the ayah, showing who is being referred to.

steps.m: to be close to or in charge of something

steps.n: Added أ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'more' of something
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific one that is more worthy/closer
Added ٰنِ to show we're talking about exactly two things
Kasra on nun marks nominative case in dual form
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added انِ to show two people are doing the action

steps.m: so they both swear an oath (connected to يَقُومَانِ - they both stand)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this action with the previous verb يَقُومَانِ (they both stand)
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. It's related to the root meaning of divinity but is treated as a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by Allah (used in swearing an oath)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show swearing by something. In this ayah, it's used with يُقْسِمَانِ (they both swear) to show they are swearing by Allah.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Connected letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Changed verb into noun meaning 'act of witnessing'
Added 'tu' ending to show this is doing the testifying/witnessing
Adds 'our' to show who the testimony belongs to
This root carries the basic meaning of truth and rightness
Adding the vowel marks makes it a proper past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and doubled the ق with ّ to make it mean 'more/most true' - comparing it to their testimony
This is a helper word that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects to the word شَهٰدَتِهِمَا (their testimony) to show the testimony is coming from them.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Added alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning 'testimony'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific act of testifying
Adding ت makes it a feminine noun
Added fatha to show this is a connecting word

steps.m: and not (in the context of وَمَا ٱعْتَدَيْنَآ - and we did not transgress)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'not' in this context since it's followed by a past tense verb (ٱعْتَدَيْنَآ). The و connects this denial to the previous oath.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Added اِعْتَ to show someone going beyond limits on purpose, like breaking rules they know about
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added long ا at the end to emphasize the meaning
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English
Added آ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the verb اعْتَدَيْنَآ (we transgressed) in this ayah.
This is the base form meaning 'when' or 'at the time'

steps.m: then/in that case (showing consequence of ٱسْتَحَقَّآ إِثْمًا - if they earned sin)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes the meaning from just 'when' to show consequence - 'then' or 'in that case'
Starting with the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or belongs to
Adding fatha at the end to help connect this word smoothly with the next word (الظَّالِمِينَ)
Adding لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Adding shadda on ل to strengthen its pronunciation because it follows another ل in the previous word (لَشَهٰدَتُنَآ)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it
Makes the word refer to many people instead of just one
Adds 'the' at the beginning and doubles the first letter to show emphasis. This word comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show elongation
Adding ل with kasra changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far
The كَ at the end is needed to complete the word - it's like a puzzle piece that makes it mean 'that over there'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison (more/closer)
Changed to final form of comparative by adding ى at end
Added the alif symbol (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: that (introduces the verb يَأْتُوا - they come/bring)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what is expected to happen - that they will bring the testimony. It makes the verb after it subjunctive (different ending).
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with ْ (sukun) on أ and ِ (kasra) on ت
Changed to plural by adding وا at the end, which replaces the ي. The ُ (damma) before و shows it's connected to the word.
Basic past tense verb form meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed verb 'witnessed' into a noun 'testimony' by adding alif after first letter and ة at end
Added definite article and shadda for sun letter
Added preposition ب and kasra ending to show connection
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is positioned above another

steps.m: on, upon (preposition showing position over وَجْهِهَآ)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is a complete word, not the start of something longer. Here it shows position over 'its face/form' (وَجْهِهَآ)

steps.m: to face or direct towards something

steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make the noun meaning 'face'
Added kasra to show this face belongs to or is connected to something
Added هَآ to show this is her face. The آ (long alif) shows we pause here when reading
This is a connecting word that gives options between two possibilities: either they will bring forth testimony properly, or they will fear that oaths will be refuted after their oaths.
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Adding present tense prefix ي to create 3rd person masculine singular present tense verb
Plural masculine subject marker indicating 'they' (masculine)
This particle connects two ideas and shows that the second part (يَأْتُوا - they bring) is the purpose or result of the first part. It's like saying 'so that' or 'in order that' in English.
Added vowels to make it 'he returned' in past tense
Combined the repeated letters with a shadda
Added تُ at beginning to show passive voice (something being done to someone/something). This verb comes after أَن (that) in the ayah.
Changes from 'right hand/side' to 'oaths' (plural) - in Arabic culture oaths were taken by raising the right hand
Added the ٌ mark to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to create a word meaning 'after' - this comes from the idea that what comes after is far from what came before
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to 'their oaths' that comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to swear or take an oath

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make the word mean 'an oath' instead of 'to swear'
Changed to broken plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple oaths
Changed the ending to show it's being used with another word (like 'of' in English)
Added pronoun suffix to show possession

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm
steps.n: Basic meaning focuses on protection and prevention of harm
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added reflexive meaning by adding اِتَّ at start
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and protect yourselves (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from 'he protected himself' to 'you all protect yourselves' - making it a command for a group
Added shadda to make this the proper name for the One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of وَٱتَّقُوا۟ - fear/be mindful of)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he heard' to 'listen!' by making it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the one and only God
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (subject of لَا يَهْدِى - does not guide)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject (doer) of the verb 'does not guide' in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (subject of لَا يَهْدِى - does not guide)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this part with what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: And Allah (subject of لَا يَهْدِى - does not guide)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a wasl alif (ٱ) since it's not pronounced separately after the و
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَهْدِى (guides) negative, changing its meaning to 'does not guide'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Added يَ at beginning to show present tense. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last root letter gets a kasra (ِ). The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word. This word is part of the phrase 'does not guide' (لا يَهْدِى)
Changed the verb concept into a noun for a group of people who stand together (like a community)
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ to show it's receiving the action of guiding. This word is what's being acted upon by يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah.

steps.m: one who departs from right way (doer)
steps.n: Changes from action 'break away' to describe someone who does this action (like changing 'run' to 'runner')

steps.m: those who break away from obedience (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Sound masculine plural form for more than two. This word is the object (in accusative case) of the verb يهدى (guides), showing who is not being guided.
Made definite with ال and final fatḥa added for grammatical case
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun for 'day'

steps.m: on the day (when يَجْمَعُ Allah will gather)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is telling us when something happens. It's connected to the verb يَجْمَعُ (will gather) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he gathers (the subject is اللَّهُ - Allah)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ). This word shows what Allah does in this ayah.

steps.m: to worship, to be devoted to
steps.n: This is the three-letter root that gives us the idea of worship and devotion
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only deity worthy of worship

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَجْمَعُ - He gathers)
steps.n: Added damma to show Allah is the one doing the action (gathering) in this verse. The alif at the start is written with a special mark (wasl) showing it connects with the previous word.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Changed from singular 'messenger' to plural by shortening middle vowel and changing end vowel
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb يَجْمَعُ (gathers)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and then he says (after Allah gathers the messengers)
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this happens after Allah gathers the messengers
This is the basic interrogative particle used to ask questions about things
Adding ذا makes the question more specific and emphatic

steps.m: what? (asking about the response given to the messengers)
steps.n: The final alif is elongated (آ) in the Quran for additional emphasis and to mark a pause in recitation
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he responded' in the past tense
Changed from 'he responded' to 'was responded to' by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowels to make it passive. The middle letter changed to ي because it's between two vowels.
Added تُمْ to show this happened to multiple people being spoken to. The ي is dropped because it comes before two consonants.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'they said' by adding وا at the end. Also, the middle letter changed from وَ to ا. This answers what happens after Allah asks them a question.
This is a negative particle that makes the word after it mean the opposite. Here it negates عِلْمَ (knowledge) to say 'no knowledge'
Changed the verb 'to know' into the noun 'knowledge' by adding specific vowel marks
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by لَا (no) in the phrase 'no knowledge'
Basic pronoun meaning 'us', 'our', or 'we' depending on how it's used

steps.m: for us (in response to قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added لَ (meaning 'for/to') at the start to show this is happening to us - connects to them saying something

steps.m: for us (in response to قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a long alif (آ) for emphasis in pronunciation. This is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English.
Added كَ at the end to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, it's addressing Allah, as shown by the next words أَنتَ عَلَّامُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (You are the Knower of the unseen).
This is a pronoun meaning 'you' when speaking to one male person. In this ayah, it's connected to the word عَلَّامُ (All-Knower) to say 'You are the All-Knower'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter changes 'know' to 'teach' intensively
Adding alif after the doubled letter creates a noun for someone who does this action extensively
Final damma shows this is the subject. In this context, it's an attribute of Allah describing His complete knowledge of all things.
Changed the internal vowels and added و to make it plural, showing multiple hidden or unseen things. This is a special plural form used for abstract concepts.

steps.m: the unseen things (possessed by عَلَّامُ - the All-Knower)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific and ِ at the end to show it belongs to عَلَّامُ (the All-Knower) in this ayah

steps.m: when (introducing a past event in قَالَ ٱللَّهُ - Allah said)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it introduces when Allah did specific things for Jesus, like supporting him with the Holy Spirit.
Added vowels to show this is about something that happened in the past. This is the basic past tense form before the middle letter changes.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes between two fathas. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added damma to show this is the subject/doer
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a standard Arabic root. It's the Arabic version of the name Jesus/Isa.

steps.m: O Jesus! (being directly addressed by Allah)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show this is a respectful way of calling out to someone. It's like saying 'O' or 'Oh' in English, but more formal and reverent, especially since Allah is addressing Jesus directly here.

steps.m: to build or establish a family
steps.n: The root becomes a plural noun meaning 'children' or 'descendants'
The root letters ب-ن-ي transform into 'son' by adding اِ at start and putting a sukun (stop mark) on ب
The word 'son' gets a special beginning (ٱ) and an 'a' sound at the end because it's receiving the action of 'he said'
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic meaning 'exalted one' or 'servant of God'. In this ayah, it's used as part of عِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ (Jesus son of Mary).
The word مَرْيَمَ (Mary) has a fatha mark at the end because she is being referred to as 'the mother of' in the phrase 'Jesus son of Mary'
Added fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Changed fathas to damma and sukuns to make it a command

steps.m: remember! (command to Muhammad ﷺ from Allah)
steps.n: Added hamzat al-wasl at beginning to help pronunciation
Added vowel marks to create the base noun form
Adds ة (ta marboota) at the end to make it mean 'one blessing' instead of 'blessings in general'
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my blessing'
Same word written two different ways - both mean 'my blessing'
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else.
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens when adding endings to words that end in ى.
Added كَ at the end to show this affects 'you'. In this ayah, Allah is speaking to Jesus (عيسى) about His favors upon him.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and upon (connected to وٰلِدَتِكَ - your mother)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) at the end to show this is connected to the word that follows it (وٰلِدَتِكَ meaning 'your mother')

steps.m: to give birth or bring into existence
steps.n: Basic meaning of bringing new life into existence
Changes the basic meaning into someone who does this action (like changing 'teach' to 'teacher')
Adds ة to make the word specifically mean 'mother' instead of just any parent
Adds ِ at the end because it follows the word على (meaning 'upon/on') which makes it genitive case
Adds كَ to show this is 'your mother'
This word connects two events in time, showing when something happened in the past. Here it introduces what Allah said to Jesus.
Basic meaning of giving strength or support
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong or intensive action
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added كَ at the end to show the action was done to 'you'
Basic meaning related to movement/spirit
The root letters were joined together and a damma (ُ) was added to make the word mean 'spirit/soul' instead of the action 'to breathe'
Added preposition بِ meaning 'with/by' at the beginning
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is connected to القدس (the Holy) to form 'Holy Spirit'
Adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) changes it from a root to a noun meaning the quality of being holy
Added 'al' to make it specific and kasra at the end because it follows the word 'spirit' (رُوحِ) in the phrase 'Spirit of the Holy'
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra effort or multiple times
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and ُ at end to show it's happening now. This word connects to الناس (the people) who are being spoken to.

steps.m: people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun for people by adding a long 'aa' sound after first letter
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which caused the ن to be doubled in pronunciation

steps.m: the people (as object of تُكَلِّمُ - you speak to)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word receives the action of speaking in the phrase 'you speak to the people'

steps.m: in (connects تُكَلِّمُ 'you speak' to ٱلْمَهْدِ 'the cradle')
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects how/where Jesus spoke - while he was in the cradle.

steps.m: to spread out smoothly, make comfortable
cradle (a place made comfortable for a baby)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun meaning a place that's made comfortable. This word appears after في (in) which requires the genitive case.

steps.m: the cradle (that Jesus spoke from as a baby)
steps.n: Added ال for definiteness and kasra ending because it comes after في (in). This refers to the specific cradle where baby Jesus miraculously spoke.

steps.m: mature adult (object of تُكَلِّمُ - you speak to)
steps.n: Changes the root into a noun meaning 'mature adult'. This describes Jesus speaking to people when he was a grown man.

steps.m: a mature adult (object of تُكَلِّمُ - you speak to)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of speaking - people are being spoken to as mature adults

steps.m: and a mature adult (object of تُكَلِّمُ - you speak to)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous description of speaking to people in the cradle, showing Jesus spoke to people both as a baby and as an adult

steps.m: when (referring to the time Allah taught Jesus)
steps.n: This is a particle that points to a specific time in the past. Here it introduces each blessing Allah gave to Jesus, which are being listed one after another

steps.m: and when (connecting to previous blessings mentioned)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this blessing to the previous ones being listed. This وَ helps create the list structure of 'and when... and when...' as Allah reminds Jesus of multiple blessings
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding a shaddah (double mark) on the middle letter changes it from 'knowing' to 'teaching a lot'
Adding تُ changes the verb from 'he taught' to 'I taught'

steps.m: I taught you intensively (object of the verb)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end means 'you' - it shows who is being taught
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding alif

steps.m: the book (object of عَلَّمْتُكَ - I taught you)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it 'the book' instead of just any book. The َ at the end shows it's the object being taught in عَلَّمْتُكَ (I taught you)
Root meaning 'wise decisions' becomes a noun meaning 'wisdom' - like changing 'to be wise' into 'wisdom'

steps.m: a specific instance or manifestation of wisdom
steps.n: Adding ة changes it from general wisdom to a specific piece or instance of wisdom
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added و to connect this word to the previous one (الكتاب - the book)

steps.m: and the wisdom (object of عَلَّمْتُكَ - I taught you)
steps.n: Added the فتحة at the end to show this word is receiving the action of teaching
This is a borrowed word from Hebrew meaning the Jewish holy book
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ت with shadda
Added وَ (and) at the beginning and َ at the end to show this is what was taught, connecting it to the verb عَلَّمْتُكَ (I taught you)

steps.m: Gospel (the holy book revealed to Jesus)
steps.n: This is a borrowed word from Greek meaning the Christian holy book

steps.m: the Gospel (as the object being taught)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start and a mark at the end to show this is the object being taught

steps.m: and the Gospel (as the object being taught)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning of the word

steps.m: when (referring to the time Allah taught Jesus)
steps.n: This is a particle that points to an important moment in the past. Here it introduces one of the favors Allah gave to Jesus that He is reminding him about.

steps.m: and when (connecting to previous mentions of Allah's favors)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this favor with the previous ones mentioned. This is part of a series of reminders about different blessings Allah gave to Jesus.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from past to present tense and shows either 'you' or 'she' is doing the action
Basic preposition meaning 'from' that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الطِّينِ (the clay). This combination means 'from the clay'
Root letters combined to make the noun meaning 'clay' as a material
Adds emphasis by doubling the middle root letter
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite. This word is the object of مِنَ (from) in this ayah.
This is the root meaning showing the basic concept
Changed root into a noun showing one specific form or shape
Adds genitive case marker to show possession/connection

steps.m: like/similar to the form/shape (in genitive case)
steps.n: Adds كَ prefix which means 'like/similar to'
Basic meaning related to flying
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the creature that flies
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific and kasra (ِ) at the end to show possession/belonging
Adds a doubling mark (ّ) on the ط to make the sound stronger
Basic concept of permission
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'permission'
Added kasra to show possession
Added ي to show first person possession (my)
Added بِ at beginning to show 'by means of' and changed final ي to ى (spelling variant)

steps.m: to blow into something, breathe out with force
steps.n: Basic action of blowing
Added vowel marks to make it 'he blew' in the past tense
Changes to present tense and second person (you). This connects to the previous part where Jesus is being addressed about creating birds from clay.
Adds فَ at beginning to show this happens after shaping the clay like a bird
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'. It has no root since it's a particle.
Added هَا which refers back to كَهَيْـَٔةِ (form/shape) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Basic concept of existence
Changed root letters to present tense by adding u-vowels (damma)

steps.m: it becomes (result of فَتَنفُخُ/then you blow)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'it' (the clay bird) is doing the action of becoming

steps.m: then it becomes (result of فَتَنفُخُ/then you blow)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of the blowing
Basic meaning related to flying
Root meaning 'to fly' becomes the noun 'bird' - like how in English 'fly' relates to 'flyer'

steps.m: a bird (as the thing being affected)
steps.n: Adds ًا (tanween) at the end to show this is a direct object - the bird is being acted upon

steps.m: to give permission or allow something

steps.n: Changed from an action (giving permission) to a thing (permission itself)
Adding بِ shows this permission is how/by what means Jesus creates. This connects to the verb تَخْلُقُ (you create) in the ayah.
The word needs a kasra (ِ) at the end because it's connected to another word after it

steps.m: by My permission (referring to Allah's permission)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is Allah's permission being given to Jesus. This is important because it shows Jesus only does these miracles with Allah's permission.
Basic root meaning related to healing or curing
Changed to Form IV pattern which shows causing something to happen
تُ shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the action
Added conjunction 'and' at beginning
Root refers to congenital blindness
This pattern (af'al) is used for physical conditions that someone is born with

steps.m: the one who is born blind
steps.n: Added the definite article 'the' to specify this particular blind person being cured

steps.m: to have a skin condition with white patches
steps.n: Root refers to skin condition with white patches

steps.m: person with white patches on skin
steps.n: أفعل pattern used for colors and physical characteristics

steps.m: the person with white patches (object of تُبْرِئُ)
steps.n: Adds definite article to specify this particular person being healed

steps.m: the person with white patches (object being healed by تُبْرِئُ)
steps.n: The َ ending shows this person is receiving the healing action
Added conjunction و
Root related to permission or authorization
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'permission'
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at end because it follows بِ
Added ى at the end to show it's 'my permission'
This is a particle that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it introduces several things Allah reminds Jesus about.

steps.m: and when (connecting to previous reminders to Jesus)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this reminder with the previous ones Allah is mentioning to Jesus

steps.m: to exit or make something exit
steps.n: Basic meaning of leaving or exiting
Changed from basic root 'exit' to 'make exit' by adding vowels - like changing 'go' to 'make go'

steps.m: you bring out (the dead, mentioned in context)
steps.n: Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. This refers to Prophet Jesus bringing out the dead by Allah's permission.
Root related to death
Changed the word to refer to people who are dead rather than the concept of death. The ى at the end makes it plural.

steps.m: the dead ones (object of تُخْرِجُ - you bring out)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific dead ones. This word is being acted upon by تُخْرِجُ (you bring out) in this ayah.
Changed the action of allowing into the noun 'permission'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'by means of' permission

steps.m: by my permission (connected to تَخْلُقُ - you create)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is Allah's permission, with kasra before it because it follows the preposition بِ

steps.m: when (referring to the time when Allah kept the Children of Israel from harming Jesus)
steps.n: This is a time particle that points to a specific past event mentioned in the next part of the ayah

steps.m: and when (connecting this event with the previous miracles mentioned)
steps.n: The و connects this event with the previous list of miracles given to Jesus that were mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense meaning

steps.m: I prevented/held back (the بَنِىٓ إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ from harming عِيسَى)
